Game.Notes: Dead or Alive (デッド オア アライブ) es un juego de lucha arcade de 1996 de Tecmo para el hardware arcade Sega Model 2 (2A CRX y 2B CRX) y el primer juego de su franquicia Dead or Alive. Fue portado a la Sega Saturn en 1997. También se lanzó una edición limitada para la Saturn que incluía un libro de arte.
Game.Title: Dead or Alive

Game.Notes: Rail Chase 2 (レールチェイス2) is the sequel to Rail Chase and was released for Sega Model 2 hardware in 1994. Like the original Rail Chase, the game features a pneumatic "Magical Bench Seat", which tilts and rocks with the in-game action to immerse players in the game.
Game.Title: Rail Chase 2

Game.Notes: Gunblade NY (ガンブレードN.Y.) is a lightgun shoot-'em-up game developed by Sega for Sega Model 2B CRX arcade hardware. It was released in 1995. The game is set in 2005, and has the player fire at terrorists around New York from a helicopter.
Game.Title: Gunblade NY

Game.Notes: Fighting Vipers (ファイティングバイパーズ) is a 3D fighting game developed by Sega AM2 for the Sega Model 2B CRX in 1995. It was ported the same year to the Sega Saturn with significantly reduced graphics.
Game.Title: Fighting Vipers

Game.Notes: Sega Ski Super G (セガスキー・スーパーＧ) is an arcade ski racing game released for Sega Model 2 hardware. It was followed by the similar Ski Champ in 1998. Players steer their racer by standing on "skis" which can both pivot left/right in a horizontal plane, as well as tilt to simulate edging. Metal "ski poles" are held on to for balance.
Game.Title: Sega Ski Super G

Game.Notes: Virtua Cop 2 (バーチャコップ２) is a light gun Sega Model 2A CRX game, released in 1995 and developed internally at Sega by their AM2 studio. It was ported to home systems on the Sega Saturn in 1996. It was released in PC in 1997 and Sega Dreamcast in 2000. It was later bundled with Virtua Cop in Virtua Cop: Elite Edition for PlayStation 2 in 2002.
Game.Title: Virtua Cop 2

Game.Notes:
Virtua Striker (バーチャストライカー) es un juego de fútbol arcade desarrollado por Sega AM2. Al igual que Virtua Racing, Virtua Fighter, Virtua Cop y más tarde Virtua Tennis, forma parte de la serie Virtua, y es el primero del conjunto de juegos Virtua Striker. Virtua Striker salió a la venta en 1995 para el hardware Sega Model 2B CRX y está ampliamente considerado como el primer juego de fútbol en 3D de la historia.
A diferencia de otros juegos que llevan el nombre de Virtua, Virtua Striker no fue portado a consolas domésticas cuando era nuevo. No se pondría oficialmente a disposición de los usuarios domésticos hasta casi veinte años después, cuando se lanzó una versión mejorada para la PlayStation Network japonesa y Xbox Live Arcade a principios de 2013.
Game.Title: Virtua Striker

Game.Notes: The House of the Dead (ザ・ハウス・オブ・ザ・デッド) is a light-gun game initially developed for Sega Model 2C CRX hardware in 1996. It is the first in the The House of the Dead series, and was ported to the Sega Saturn and Windows PC in 1998 due to its success. The game is a rail shoot-em-up, where you must shoot zombies.
Game.Title: The House of the Dead

Game.Notes: Behind Enemy Lines (ビハインド・エネミーラインズ) is a Sega Model 2 arcade light gun game. Behind Enemy Lines is a first person rail shooter where the player controls a machine gun mounted on a jeep driving through enemy territory. The game consists of four missions that all involve infiltrating and destroying various enemy facilities. The setting is modern and enemies include simple foot soldiers, light vehicles such as jeeps and humvees and heavy vehicles like tanks and helicopters. The game can be played by one or two players each controlling a machine gun.
Game.Title: Behind Enemy Lines

Game.Notes: Super GT 24h is a 3D arcade racing game developed and published by Jaleco for Sega Model 2B CRX hardware. It was originally released in 1996, with a watered down Sega Saturn port appearing in Japan in 1998. The Saturn version of the game is known as GT24.
Game.Title: Super GT 24h

Game.Notes:
Sonic Championship is a 3D fighting game, featuring characters from the Sonic the Hedgehog franchise. The background story is that Doctor Eggman built the Death Egg II, a new version of his facility that appeared in previous games. Stopping him requires Chaos Emeralds, and various individuals including heroes like Sonic the Hedgehog and Miles "Tails" Prower participate in a fighting tournament to gather them. In total there are eight playable characters (nine in later console releases) and two bosses (Metal Sonic and Doctor Eggman).
Gameplay is mostly typical for a versus fighter, it is a one-on-one fight on a best two out of three basis. Players use jumps, punches, kicks and special weapons. Bean the Dynamite for example, a duck-like character who makes his debut in the Sonic universe, uses bombs. Blocking is done by making use of a limited supply of barriers. The whole action is illustrated in a comical fashion though, like one would expect from a Sonic game. A round is won when a player managed to deplete an opponent's health, unless the timer runs out, in which case the character with the most energy left is the winner. Environments are 3D closed arenas, but the gameplay is mostly 2D, allowing limited sidestep maneuvers.
Game.Title: Sonic Championship

Game.Notes: Dynamite Baseball 97 (ダイナマイトベースボール９７) es un juego arcade de béisbol en 3-D lanzado únicamente en Japón y secuela de Dynamite Baseball.
Game.Title: Dynamite Baseball 97

Game.Notes:
Motor Raid (モーターレイド) es un juego arcade de Sega Model 2 de Sega AM1. Es un juego de carreras, en el que el jugador conduce motos futuristas a través de diferentes planetas. El jugador también puede golpear a sus oponentes. La mayoría (si no todas) las unidades de Motor Raid se vendieron como kits de conversión para Manx TT Super Bike, lo que hace que este juego sea algo raro. La versión de doble cabina es la versión estándar del juego. Se pueden conectar hasta 4 armarios "gemelos" para 8 jugadores.
La versión Deluxe ("DX") sólo tiene un armario para un piloto, pero un monitor más grande. Los armarios "DX" y "gemelos" no se pueden enlazar.
Game.Title: Motor Raid: Twin

Game.Notes: Desert Tank (デザートタンク) is a third-person shooter arcade game released for the Sega Model 2 in 1994. In it, the player controls a tank and blows things up. The game was developed by Sega AM2 and produced by Yu Suzuki. It was developed in cooperation with Martin Marietta, a military contractor, and aimed to be realistic. It has never been brought to home consoles.
Game.Title: Desert Tank

Game.Notes:
Sega Rally Championship (セガラリー・チャンピオンシップ), also known simply as Sega Rally or occasionally by the extended title of Sega Rally Championship 1995, is an arcade racing game developed by Sega AM3 for Sega Model 2A CRX arcade hardware. It was released in 1995 to critical acclaim, and is the first in the Sega Rally series.
Sega Rally is an off-road racing game, in which players drive rally cars across one of four tracks as quickly as possible. Like prior Sega arcade racing games, Sega Rally enforces a strict time limit and a checkpoint system - success is measured by how fast the player can navigate the three standard tracks before either crossing the final finish line or retiring due to the timer running out.
The game came in three cabinet models with the upright version being the standard. There are also the Twin (two sit-down player positions) and DX model cabinets. The DX cabinet is a single-player sit-down cabinet with a larger game screen. The Twin and DX cabinets contain an additional handbrake that is needed in the tight hairpin turns. The game supports up to four players using cabinets linked together.
Game.Title: Sega Rally Championship

Game.Notes: Virtua Fighter 2 (バーチャファイター２) es un juego de lucha desarrollado por Sega AM2 y publicado por Sega como secuela directa de Virtua Fighter. Es, como su nombre indica, el segundo juego de la serie Virtua Fighter, y fue lanzado originalmente para el hardware arcade Sega Modelo 2A CRX en 1994. Virtua Fighter 2 fue conocido por sus revolucionarios gráficos y animaciones, que renderizaban personajes y fondos en 3D con mapeado de texturas filtrado e introducían la tecnología de animación por captura de movimiento en la industria del videojuego.
Game.Title: Virtua Fighter 2

Game.Notes: Virtua Cop (バーチャコップ) is an arcade shooter game created by Sega AM2 for Sega Model 2 hardware in 1994. It is the first in the Virtua Cop series. It was one of the first light-gun games to take place in a 3D world, as the first light-gun shooter to use 3D polygons. Sega advertised it as the "world's first texture mapped, polygon action game".
Game.Title: Virtua Cop

Game.Notes: Last Bronx, subtitled Last Bronx: Tokyo Bangaichi (ラストブロンクス -東京番外地-) in Japan, is a 3D versus fighting game developed by Sega AM3 for Sega Model 2B CRX arcade hardware.
Game.Title: Last Bronx

Game.Notes: Sega Water Ski (セガ・ウォータースキー) is a Sega Model 2 arcade game which simulates water skiing. As a result, the player controls the game with their feet. It should not be confused with Water Ski, a much earlier arcade game by Taito. Sega Water Ski has never seen a home release.
Game.Title: Sega Water Ski

Game.Notes:
Manx TT Super Bike (マンクスＴＴ スーパーバイク) is a Sega Model 2A CRX 3D arcade motorbike racing game developed by Sega AM3 and Sega AM4. It was initially released in 1995. It is a motorcycle racing game built for the Sega Model 2 arcade board. Up to 8 players can race in this game if enough cabinets are linked together (4 Twin two rider cabinets or 8 Deluxe single rider cabinet units). The Deluxe cabinet includes a larger single unit 50-inch projector screen.
The game's setting is the Isle of Man TT - the world-famous and demanding motorcycle racing event held on the Isle of Man. There are two courses to race on: the Laxey Coast course for novices and the more difficult TT ("Tourist Trophy") Course for veteran players. While the TT Course is based on the actual course on the Isle of Man, the Laxey Coast is a fictional course designed by the game developers, though its scenery is drawn from the Isle of Man.
The arcade game was known at the time for its impressive graphics and innovative cabinet. Many arcade motorcycle games incorporated a bike-like machine that tilted so the player could maneuver the on-screen bike through the physical "bike" (pioneered by another Sega game, Hang-On); to do this, the player would need to push their feet against the floor. The Manx TT machine, however, was sensitive enough to tilt just from the rider shifting their weight, allowing the player to keep their feet on the machine and use their body weight to control the on-screen bike, making the game feel more realistic. Many of the unsold cabinets were converted into Motor Raid, a futuristic Model 2 motorcycle racing game released in 1997.
Game.Title: Manx TT Superbike: DX

Game.Notes: Sega Touring Car Championship (セガツーリングカーチャンピオンシップ) is a Sega Model 2C CRX arcade racing game developed by AM Annex in 1996. It is similar to Sega Rally Championship in design, but uses touring cars instead of rally cars.
Game.Title: Sega Touring Car Championship

Game.Notes: Indy 500 Twin es un juego de carreras desarrollado por Sega AM1 y publicado por Sega en arcades para la plataforma arcade Sega Model 2B CRX en 1995. Está basado en la carrera anual de las 500 Millas de Indianápolis que se celebra en Indiana, EE.UU., y en algunos aspectos es una evolución del anterior Virtua Racing. Indy 500 Twin (una cabina doble para dos jugadores) es la versión estándar del juego. También existe una versión para un solo jugador.
Game.Title: INDY 500 Twin

Game.Notes:
Daytona USA (デイトナＵＳＡ) is a Sega Model 2 arcade racing game developed by Sega AM2. It was initially given a limited release in Japan in 1993 to gauge public reaction to the new model hardware before its full, international release the following March.
It is loosely based on the NASCAR racing event at the Daytona International Speedway, located in real life in Daytona Beach, Florida in the United States of America. Daytona USA and all of its sequels and rereleases (except Sega Racing Classic, in which case someone else already held the arcade license) have been officially sponsored by the International Speedway Corporation.
Game.Title: Daytona USA

Game.Notes: Top Skater is a skateboarding game that uses a physical skate board controller. It has fast and over the top gameplay and a visual style similar to the later Crazy Taxi games. The focus is on performing stunts against a time limit.
Game.Title: Top Skater

Game.Notes: Pilot Kids (パイロット キッズ) is a side-scrolling 2.5D shoot-'em-up released by Psikyo for Sega Model 2 arcade hardware in 1999. It was the last official Model 2 game to be released.
Game.Title: Pilot Kids

Game.Notes: Dynamite Cop, known as Dynamite Deka 2 (ダイナマイト刑事２) in Japan, is a beat-'em-up game created by Sega AM1. It was released for all three versions of the Sega Model 2 arcade hardware in 1998 (the only game to run on all Model 2 boards) before subsequently being ported to the Sega Dreamcast the following year. It is a sequel to Die Hard Arcade (Dynamite Deka in Japan), though unlike its predecessor, lacks the Die Hard film license. It was soon followed up by Asian Dynamite.
Game.Title: Dynamite Cop!

Game.Notes: Virtual On: Cyber Troopers (電脳戦機バーチャロン, Dennō Senki Bācharon) es un videojuego de 1996 desarrollado y publicado por Sega. Se trata de un juego de lucha en 3D protagonizado por robots que salió a la venta en salones recreativos y para Sega Saturn y PC, tanto en América como en Japón. También se lanzaron versiones en línea para dos jugadores en América y Japón para la Sega Saturn utilizando los servicios NetLink y XBAND. Inicialmente, el juego iba a salir a la venta bajo el título "Virtual On" en Japón y "Cyber Troopers" en Norteamérica, pero finalmente estos dos nombres se combinaron en un único título para ambas regiones.
Game.Title: Cyber Troopers Virtual-On

Game.Notes:
Another 3rd person perspective arcade flight sim by Sega, similar to their classic After Burner in gameplay but featuring new gameplay mechanics and an entirely polygonal engine.
As in most games of it's type your objective is to clear a number of stages by shooting anything that moves, but at the end of each stage you can select where to go next thanks to branching paths a-la Darius, which coupled with 4 different planes to select give the game an increased gameplay value. No multiple lives are given, but you get a life bar (which can be replenished in-flight) to help you outlast your enemies.
Game.Title: Sky Target

Game.Notes:
Manx TT Super Bike (マンクスＴＴ スーパーバイク) is a Sega Model 2A CRX 3D arcade motorbike racing game developed by Sega AM3 and Sega AM4. It was initially released in 1995. It is a motorcycle racing game built for the Sega Model 2 arcade board. Up to 8 players can race in this game if enough cabinets are linked together (4 Twin two rider cabinets or 8 Deluxe single rider cabinet units). The Deluxe cabinet includes a larger single unit 50-inch projector screen. The twin model two player cabinet was the standard model; hence, also referred to as Manx TT Superbike: Twin
The game's setting is the Isle of Man TT - the world-famous and demanding motorcycle racing event held on the Isle of Man. There are two courses to race on: the Laxey Coast course for novices and the more difficult TT ("Tourist Trophy") Course for veteran players. While the TT Course is based on the actual course on the Isle of Man, the Laxey Coast is a fictional course designed by the game developers, though its scenery is drawn from the Isle of Man.
The arcade game was known at the time for its impressive graphics and innovative cabinet. Many arcade motorcycle games incorporated a bike-like machine that tilted so the player could maneuver the on-screen bike through the physical "bike" (pioneered by another Sega game, Hang-On); to do this, the player would need to push their feet against the floor. The Manx TT machine, however, was sensitive enough to tilt just from the rider shifting their weight, allowing the player to keep their feet on the machine and use their body weight to control the on-screen bike, making the game feel more realistic. Many of the unsold cabinets were converted into Motor Raid, a futuristic Model 2 motorcycle racing game released in 1997.
Game.Title: Manx TT Superbike

Game.Notes: WaveRunner (ウェーブランナー) is a waterski racing game released for Sega Model 2 arcade hardware. It was followed by WaveRunner GP.
Game.Title: Wave Runner

Game.Notes: Over Rev (オーバーレブ) is a racing game developed by Jaleco for Sega's Model 2 arcade board. It features four nicely rendered tracks across the Greater Tokyo Area. Although it has some serious issues with driving and collision physics, if you stick with the game long enough to overcome its intricacies, it can be pretty rewarding. Like Jaleco's other Model 2 racer, Super GT 24h, you'll want to use an automatic transmission.
Game.Title: Over Rev

Game.Notes:
Zero Gunner es un juego de disparos desarrollado por Psikyo y publicado en 1997. El juego arcade permite el movimiento en ocho direcciones mediante un joystick y admite hasta dos jugadores a la vez. Destacaba por su mecánica de fijación de objetivos, que permitía a los jugadores girar alrededor de los mismos. El juego fue sucedido por una secuela, Zero Gunner 2 para la Sega NAOMI/Dreamcast.
Trama En la fecha futura de 2016, se inicia la ley marcial en todo el mundo cuando una organización terrorista generalizada derrocó y tomó el control de las autoridades militares del mundo. Un grupo de pilotos de helicóptero as se reúnen en secreto en un escuadrón de fuerzas especiales llamado ZERO para viajar por todo el mundo y destruir a las fuerzas terroristas ocupadas.
Game.Title: Zero Gunner

AlternateName.Name: セガ・ウォータースキー
